postData.contentHtml:
The AI gold rush has sent tech stocks soaring, with investors eagerly chasing the next big winner. But amidst the clamor for data-center darlings and chipmakers, a seasoned voice on Wall Street is urging a different approach. Jim Cramer, the outspoken host of CNBC's Mad Money, is advising investors to look beyond the obvious and consider a familiar name with a surprisingly strong AI story: IBM.

While initial reports hinted at AI stocks boasting astronomical gains of 460% and 1,300% since 2023, the deeper dive into Cramer's recent recommendations reveals a strategic pivot. His focus has shifted to companies that offer a more grounded, yet equally compelling, investment thesis in the burgeoning AI landscape.

Cramer's pitch for IBM is rooted in several key factors that set it apart from its high-flying peers:

  • Attractive Valuation: In a market where many AI stocks trade at forward earnings multiples north of 40x, IBM stands out with a more modest 22x forward P/E. This valuation, described by Cramer as "almost quaint," suggests significant upside potential for investors seeking value in the AI space.
  • Robust Financial Performance: IBM delivered a strong Q1 2026, beating both revenue and EPS estimates. The company reported impressive growth in its Software segment (up 11.3%), with Red Hat contributing 13% and Data growing 19%. Perhaps most notably, IBM Z mainframe revenue surged by an astonishing 51% year-over-year, demonstrating the company's foundational strength.
  • Accelerating Generative AI Business: Far from being a bystander in the AI revolution, IBM's generative AI book of business has already crossed $12.5 billion inception-to-date. This accelerating segment, with significant contributions from both Consulting and Software, indicates a tangible and growing involvement in cutting-edge AI applications. IBM's fully populated Z mainframes can handle an estimated 450 billion inferences a day, showcasing its capacity for enterprise-level AI workloads.
  • Reliable Dividend: Unlike many growth-focused AI companies, IBM offers the stability of a consistent dividend. The company recently raised its dividend to $1.69 per share, marking its 31st consecutive annual increase, a "ballast" that provides an income floor for investors.
  • Analyst Endorsement: Bank of America recently upgraded IBM, raising its price target to $330, citing software strength, Confluent synergies, and the company's dividend record.

Of course, no investment comes without its caveats. Bearish concerns include whether AI is merely cannibalizing older consulting services, given that consulting revenue grew only 4% despite holding the largest share of IBM's AI business. However, Cramer's overall call remains coherent: a compelling dividend yield, a reasonable forward multiple, a rapidly growing AI business, and a resilient mainframe cycle.

For investors looking for AI exposure without the extreme volatility and elevated valuations of some market darlings, Jim Cramer's recommendation of IBM offers a thoughtful alternative. It's a bet on a legacy tech giant that is quietly, but effectively, carving out its significant role in the artificial intelligence era.
postData.title: Jim Cramer Bets on 'Boring' AI: Why IBM is His Latest Recommendation
postData.summary: Jim Cramer, known for his bold market calls, is now steering investors away from the typical AI darlings towards a surprising legacy tech giant. Discover why IBM is catching Cramer's eye with its attractive valuation, strong financials, and robust generative AI business.

sources.title: Cramer Says Forget the Data-Center Darlings. This ‘Boring’ AI Stock at 22x Is the Buy.
